Watch: Iran war: Iran just needs to play the longer game, says Trita Parsi
items.summary: Iran doesn’t need a quick victory—it just needs to outlast its adversaries, says expert Trita Parsi. In this analysis of the Iran war, Parsi argues that Tehran’s strategy is rooted in patience, resilience, and long-term pressure rather than immediate wins.

items.summary: Russia has announced a temporary ban on gasoline exports from April 1 to July 31, 2026, to stabilise domestic fuel supply and control prices amid volatility caused by the West Asia crisis.

items.title: Iran-Israel war: A day‑by‑day rundown of the escalating crisis
items.summary: A joint U.S.-Israel attack on Iran and the killing of Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei have plunged West Asia into a fast‑spreading war, with Tehran and its allies launching retaliatory strikes across Israel, the Gulf and U.S. bases.

items.title: Situating Nepal’s current political moment in the long history of feudalism to republican democracy
items.summary: Nepal’s history since 1950 has seen feudal rule, absolute  monarchy, civil war, and a republic — each transition  promising transformation but leaving most fundamental  constraints untouched. The RSP’s 2026 landslide win after a  massive but violent Gen Z movement faces the same test

items.title: Federalism in Nepal: Contested past, controversial present, and challenged future
items.summary: More than a decade after the 2015 Constitution created  seven provinces following a tumultuous struggle for state  restructuring, Nepal’s federalism remains an area of political  contest. Provinces are starved of fiscal autonomy, bypassed  by Kathmandu and now it is up to the triumphant RSP, a party  that once refused to even contest provincial elections, to  resuscitate it.

items.title: A tectonic shift in Nepal’s politics
items.summary: The RSP’s stunning victory in the wake of the Gen Z protests  revealed a yearning for change across Nepal and even beyond

items.title: The September uprising: How Nepal’s youth changed the game
items.summary: A digitally sparked demonstration overthrew an elected  government and upended Nepali politics. Six months on,  the country now looks for real change — with Himalayan  expectations.

items.title: Political metamorphosis of contemporary Nepal
items.summary: Nepal’s traditional political parties, both the Nepali Congress  and the many communist parties, came to life during the  Cold War era and were structured around feudal-style vertical  hierarchies. Today, after the March elections, they have been  swept aside by a generation that has organised horizontally,  communicating through popular rap songs and social media  and which views politics through the lens of individual  aspiration rather than collective ideology. This mobilisation  helped a relatively new force to win the elections, but will it  be able to govern?

items.title: A tide in the affairs of Nepal
items.summary: India and Nepal need to reimagine, perhaps to ‘repurpose’, an age old relationship, so that it can fulfil its real potential in a geopolitical and geo-economic landscape so completely different from the one it  inherited when the British left India in 1947

items.title: The Hindu e-book on Nepal’s new political moment
items.summary: In an era defined by digital mobilisation and anti-establishment anger, Nepal’s new political moment is an essential and illuminating read.

items.title: Meloni’s referendum gamble backfires

items.title: Balendra Shah takes oath as Nepal’s Prime Minister
